replies.author: @ChaptermasterPedroKantor-kv5yw
replies.text: For that alone I would never go back to Disney Soy Wars, even if they un-soyed it and made it good again. It would be like going back to an abusive spouse after he or she sent you some flowers and chocolates. We know that Disney can apologize for its behavior, they proved that when Bob Chapek was forced to apologize to the LGHDTV cult. If they don't do it to us for having called us BAD PEOPLE FROM WW2 GERMANY, then they just don't really care.

replies.author: @MikeDaPatriot

replies.text: ​@ChaptermasterPedroKantor-kv5yw I'm not going back to Star Wars unless it is cleansed of CW08 and Ahsoka Tano. Whoever convinced Lucas CW08 as a good idea needs to be named and shamed.

replies.author: @ChaptermasterPedroKantor-kv5yw
replies.text: @ShannonCarter55 I fully consider Lucas hiring Phony Filoni as easily his 5th greatest mistake. The other 4 being erasing the original theatrical cuts from existence, making the Prequels the way he did it, selling LF to DIsney and hiring KK as his successor. The 1 good thing to have come from the Ahsoka series is that it finally proved to many in the fandom that Phony Filoni is not their savior.

replies.author: @ChaptermasterPedroKantor-kv5yw
replies.text: @MikeDaPatriot I reckon it has been the decades long trend of Hollywood getting ever more leftwing, and of course the long march through the institutions by university educated marxists. This has been LONG in the make, at least since the 60's, when College Boomers embraced marxism. Those that stayed in academia worked their way through the academic ladder, until they became deans and professors, and then indoctrinated the next generation. We thought that those crazy college kids would grow up once they left academia and entered the real work force. As it turned out they didn't. They worked their way up the corporate ladder, and now they run companies. Which is kinda super funny and ironic, that marxists now run supposedly capitalist corporations. I don't think old Karl had conceived of that.

replies.author: @NeverAStraightAnswer
replies.text: @MikeDaPatriotthey have an agenda to shape culture. It doesn’t matter about movie quality or audience anymore, they simply have marching orders to change society through the stories and imagery in movies and television

They are going to focus the trailer on he-man, bringing in the old audience and once you start watching the movie its going to be all about everyone else, and he will be cucked in one way or another. author: @Falk1987
text: I watched the He-Man tv show as a kid, back in the 90s, and I liked it. So I was aware of He-Man, and I knew about his sword and the green cat, but I knew nothing of the lore or really anything about that universe. I knew more about Star Wars then He-Man and I was never a big fan of Star Wars to begin with. So the fact that a He-Man movie is the most anticipated movie of the year, one that even I am excited to go watch, is just crazy. Do you realize the damage you have to do to a brand for that to happen? Disney really did fuck up with Star Wars didn't they?

author: @farharbor3178
text: Disney's problem (and Mando/Grogus) is a psychological one.   Humans are drawn to expanding potential.  They're turned off and disappointed by inevitable decline ... whether they realize it or not.   Because almost everything they're doing has to basically lead toward Luke Failing, The Republic Failing; Rey just showing up .... it makes it psychologically hard to care (even for the people who profess to like the Sequels).   The ST (and by extension all of Disney Star Wars) is emotionally/dramatically inert.   It's all so comprehensively underwhelming that's it become very very difficult to care about any of this even if you were to try to be invested.

author: @trevorwilliams2482
text: Trailers are supposed to get you hyped for an upcoming film -- Period. Now saying the obvious looking at the numbers tells me that Star Wars fans might has SOME interest in it ( Mando Movie ) at best it's a passing interest and even those fans are praying they do not screw it up ( too bad ). While the Masters of the Universe trailer while flawed like you said looks fun and hooks you and makes a viewer go -- hmm maybe I will go check this out when it released. While Mando movie looks ok ( maybe ) it looks like a TV show moved to the big screen not much if anything hooks the viewer even IF the franchise was in a better state the trailer was still generic and Star Wars is not supposed to be generic -- period.

replies.text: Maybe, but as Mando S3 showed, people are over the cute Baby Yoda thing. And why would people who never bothered to watch Mando on a behind a paywall streamer go watch a Mando movie in theater? That movie better be costing less then 100 mln to make just to break even with marketing at 400 mln. And lets also be honest, only TLJ came in on budget, 300 mln, to make. Both TFA and Rise of Palpatine crossed the 550 mln mark to make thanks to excessive reshoots. They basically made Rise of Palpy twice. So compared to the other movies cheaper then may be relative. If it costs anything over 100 mln to make it will likely lose money. And Disney does not know anymore how to make cheap movies.

text: Yeah, was disappointed with the He/Him sign for He-Man....booooo. On the fence about the movie. Not really digging the blend of modern times to He-Man's world. Will say though, He-Man's world does look very good from the cartoon and Skeletor looks wicked. We shall see, but not holding my breath just yet.

author: @jaredfoss5668
text: Masters of the Universe looks promising. Disney Star Wars looks dead. I think people are hungry for their childhood heroes like He-Man to be brought into the 21st century in various forms of media that respects the past and creates something new and exciting that people of all ages can enjoy and share together. Star Wars hasn't done that for a long time. I like the Mandalorian and Grogu, but the trailer didn't excite me at all. Masters of the Universe did. I'm keeping my fingers crossed that MOTU is a good movie.

author: @PhilippVoegtli
text:
Hollywood takes film franchises that are very popular, such as Star Wars, Star Trek, or Marvel, and then produces films that are not made for the fans, and then wonders why the fans are disappointed and these films flop at the box office. The best example is Star Trek: Starfleet Academy. I don't know what that's supposed to be, but it's not Star Trek. Can someone explain it to me? 
Judging by the movie trailer, that doesn't seem to be the case with Masters of the Universe. You can tell that there are fans behind it who know what they're doing. I sincerely hope so. It would be a great shame if Masters of the Universe is another disappointment.

author: @bosskbounty
text:
They sprayed him with pepper spray, tackled him, beat him in the head with the pepper spray container, disarmed him of his legally possessed firearm (never removed from its holster), and shot him in the back. More than 10 times.

All because he was trying to help a woman to her feet after SHE was pepper sprayed and shoved to the ground. His last words, directed at the fallen woman were, “Are you ok?”

The only thing Alex Pretti brandished was his camera.

It was an execution.
